evals.prompt: I've got a legacy PowerBuilder module in '10-workstreams/pb-migration/BOP Registration' and our legacy schema inventory is the YAML under '80-reference/legacy-db/income-dw-analysis/phase1_source_tables.yaml'. I can't get database access yet. Which tables and columns does this module use, and can you produce something I can load into OpenMetadata?
evals.expected_output: Runs the extractor against bopindex.pbl with the inventory; produces a finished <Module>_OpenMetadata.yaml + .md covering the BOP tables (taxpayer, bop, bopyearmember, partner, contact, coincentive, whitefile, etc.) with app->table lineage and DRAFT business descriptions; prunes framework false-positives; maps the abbreviation prefixes; marks output DRAFT with a verification note.

evals.prompt: Document the data this PowerBuilder app touches so the catalogue team can review it. The .pbl is at ./Bopindex/bopindex.pbl. I don't have a table list to cross-check against.
evals.expected_output: Runs the extractor with no inventory (fallback mode), is appropriately more cautious about the table list, surfaces tables seen in DML/DataWindow contexts, groups columns, writes a markdown review sheet, and flags that the table list needs tighter confirmation because no inventory was supplied.

evals.prompt: We have ~70 legacy PowerBuilder modules to bring into OpenMetadata before DcP3. Can you show me the workflow on one module and what the output looks like, so my team can repeat it on the rest?
evals.expected_output: Explains the run-script-then-finish-by-judgement workflow, runs it on the available module as the demonstration, produces the YAML+MD, and points at the per-module repeatability (one command + the finishing steps), including the verification gate before publishing.
